title: rosa hirta subsp. globata
intro: Rosa hirta subsp. globata, also known as the Sweetbrier or Scotch Rose, is a type of wild rose that belongs to the Rosa genus of flowering plants. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat digestive issues and skin irritations, and is rich in antioxidants and vitamin C. The health benefits of Rosa hirta subsp. globata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve skin health, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Rosa hirta subsp. globata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to soothe digestive issues, skin irritations, and other health concerns.
